segments.text: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he went on from there to teach and preach in the towns of Galilee.
When John, who was in prison, heard about the deeds of the Messiah, he sent his disciples
to ask him,
Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else?
segments.speaker: John's disciples

segments.text: Jesus replied,
Go back and report to John what you hear and see:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, those who have leprosy are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is proclaimed to the poor.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blessed is anyone who does not stumble on account of me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As John's disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowd about John:
What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swayed by the wind?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If not, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? No, those who wear fine clothes are in kings' palaces.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: This is the one about whom it is written: 'I will send my messenger ahead of you, who will prepare your way before you.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has not risen anyone greater than John the Baptist; yet whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subjected to violence, and violent people have been raiding it.
For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Whoever has ears, let them hear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: 'We played the pipe for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say,
He has a demon.
segments.speaker: Unspecified people

segments.text: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say,
Here is a glutton and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners.
segments.speaker: Unspecified people

segments.text: But wisdom is proved right by her deeds.
Then Jesus began to denounce the towns in which most of his miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.
Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted to the heavens? No, you will go down to Hades. For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: The Father Revealed in the Son

segments.text: At that time Jesus said,
I praise you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes, Father, for this is what you were pleased to do.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All things have been committed to me by my Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For my yoke is easy and my burden is light.
segments.speaker: Jesus
